summary: Iteration 160 testing complete: Barrister Quick Brief PDF export, database index initialization, and dependency audit. All 17 backend tests passed. Frontend Quick Brief button verified with correct data-testid and emerald styling.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/cases/{case_id}/reports/barrister-quick-brief endpoint correctly generates PDF with ReportLab
PDF returns 200 with application/pdf content type and Content-Disposition header for download
PDF starts with %PDF- magic bytes confirming valid PDF format
PDF size is ~3.4KB which is reasonable for a 2-page brief
Database startup creates indexes for 30+ collections including users, cases, reports, grounds_of_merit, documents, pipeline_tasks, user_sessions, payments
Frontend Quick Brief button has data-testid='barrister-quick-brief-button' and emerald-700 styling
Button correctly calls handleQuickBrief which downloads PDF via axios with blob responseType
seed_data_creation: None - used existing case case_f8bf63e9dcbe with completed barrister report
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 160 verified: (1) Barrister Quick Brief PDF export at GET /api/cases/{case_id}/reports/barrister-quick-brief - generates 2-page PDF with Counsel Synthesis, Priority Order, top 3 grounds, viability ratings, and disclaimer. Note: Counsel Synthesis shows 'not available' for existing reports generated before the prompt was added - this is expected. (2) Database index initialization at startup for 30+ collections. (3) Frontend Quick Brief button in BarristerView.jsx with emerald styling and Zap icon. Test case: case_f8bf63e9dcbe (Joshua Scott Homann - Murder case with 10 grounds and completed barrister report).
